properties.name: The Commons
properties.description: A vast, vaulted plaza that once served as the heart of shipboard life, now hushed and shadowed. Dust-shrouded kiosks and tiered seating circles are arranged around a dry central fountain, while high above, a dead artificial sun-strip bisects the ceiling. The walls are a patchwork of styles: elegant murals of a forgotten Earth compete with jagged, neon-painted symbols and hanging woven tapestries.
properties.shortDescription: the vast, dark expanse of the Commons

properties.name: Exit east
properties.description: Leads back to Junction C.

properties.name: The Promenade
properties.description: A wide walkway lined with the hollow shells of storefronts and workshops.
properties.destinationIntent: A commercial district with storefronts, some boarded up, some displaying strange handcrafted goods. Focus on the 'The Guild of Glass' or similar artisanal faction.

properties.name: Residential Arch
properties.description: A towering archway leads toward the dense, honeycomb-like residential stacks.
properties.destinationIntent: The living quarters. Cramped but highly personalized modular housing. Evidence of high-density living and vertical gardens.

properties.name: The Weaver Statue
properties.description: A fountain statue carved from a translucent, bone-like resin. It depicts a figure with four arms weaving what looks like a DNA helix out of copper wire, though the wire has been stripped away in several places by scavengers.
properties.takeRefusal: The statue is fused to the fountain base, a monument to a philosophy of biological design.

properties.name: Dormant Festival Cart
properties.description: A brightly painted wooden cart, draped in grey dust cloths. It smells faintly of dried spices and ozone. A hand-painted sign on the side reads 'Zola's Synthetics: Flavors of the Old World'.

properties.name: The Commons
properties.description: A vast, vaulted plaza that served as the ship's social heart. Grand tiered balconies overlook a floor strewn with dust-covered kiosks and circular benches, while the ceiling—once a shimmering sky-simulation—is now a dark, starlit void of dead pixels. Faded banners of competing guilds hang limply from the railings, their once-vibrant heraldry now gray with age.

properties.name: Exit east
properties.description: Leads back to Junction C.

properties.name: High Street
properties.description: A wide, shop-lined boulevard that disappears into the gloom.
properties.destinationIntent: A commercial district with storefronts representing different eras and odd ship-specific industries.

properties.name: The Sunken Garden
properties.description: A flight of stairs leads down into a darkened pit where the smell of damp earth persists.
properties.destinationIntent: A multi-level indoor park that has gone feral, featuring bioluminescent fungi and strange adapted flora.

properties.name: Information Kiosk
properties.description: A waist-high pedestal carved from a single block of synthetic marble. Its screen is dark, but a handwritten sign taped to the side reads: 'The Festival of Third Contact is postponed until the air scrubbers are fixed.'
properties.takeRefusal: The kiosk is bolted firmly into the deck plating.

properties.name: Dormant Sweeper Bot
properties.description: A low, disc-shaped machine resting in a thick pile of its own uncollected dust. A small knitted hat has been placed atop its sensor dome, a whimsical touch from a long-gone owner.

properties.name: The Commons
properties.description: A vast, vaulted plaza that once served as the ship's heart. The ceiling is lost in shadow, but the floor is a patchwork of mismatched tiles and faded carpets, each section reflecting a different neighborhood's aesthetic. Dozens of kiosks and modular dwellings stand like silent sentinels, most draped in grey dust cloths that stir in a phantom breeze.

properties.name: East toward Junction C
properties.description: A wide passage leading toward the bow. The guide strips here split into multiple colors. A sign reads THE COMMONS but the lights beyond are dark.

properties.name: Exit east
properties.description: Leads back to Junction C.

properties.name: The Promenade
properties.description: A wide, glass-walled walkway that overlooks the lower tiers of the Commons.
properties.destinationIntent: A high-end shopping and social district with more refined, artistic architecture and possibly defunct automated storefronts.

properties.name: The Warrens
properties.description: The open plaza gives way to a dense thicket of repurposed shipping containers and stacked living pods.
properties.destinationIntent: A high-density residential area, messy and crowded with evidence of a DIY aesthetic and jury-rigged power lines.

properties.name: The Silent Weeper
properties.description: A fountain statue carved from a single block of recycled polymer. It depicts a figure holding a leaking jug, but instead of water, a slow-moving, bioluminescent algae drips from the vessel, casting a faint green glow on the surrounding dry basin.
properties.takeRefusal: The statue is bolted to the deck plates and weighs more than a hibernation pod.

properties.name: Fragmented Bulletin Board
properties.description: A cork-like surface covered in layers of physical paper, digital sticky notes, and hand-painted signs. Most are sun-bleached or corrupted, but a few fragments remain legible.
properties.takeRefusal: It's a heavy slab of communal history; you can't just walk off with it.

properties.name: The Commons
properties.description: A vast, vaulted plaza that once served as the ship's heart. The ceiling is lost in shadow, but the floor is a patchwork of mismatched tiles and faded carpets, each section reflecting a different neighborhood's aesthetic. Dozens of kiosks and modular dwellings stand like silent sentinels, most draped in grey dust cloths that stir in a phantom breeze.

scenery.description: This particular unit appears to have been a dispensary for 'Memory-Silk,' featuring a glass counter etched with the faded logo of a blooming lotus. Inside the display, a robotic mannequin with three slender arms remains frozen in the middle of a delicate weaving gesture.
scenery.rejection: The kiosk is hermetically sealed behind a layer of reinforced polymer, and the vendor-bot ignores your rhythmic tapping on the glass.

properties.name: The Commons
properties.description: A vast, vaulted plaza that once served as the ship's heart. The ceiling is lost in shadow, but the floor is a patchwork of mismatched tiles and faded carpets, each section reflecting a different neighborhood's aesthetic. Dozens of kiosks and modular dwellings stand like silent sentinels, most draped in grey dust cloths that stir in a phantom breeze.

scenery.description: This particular unit appears to have been a dispensary for 'Memory-Silk,' featuring a glass counter etched with the faded logo of a blooming lotus. Inside the display, a robotic mannequin with three slender arms remains frozen in the middle of a delicate weaving gesture.
scenery.rejection: The kiosk is hermetically sealed behind a layer of reinforced polymer, and the vendor-bot ignores your rhythmic tapping on the glass.

properties.name: Hibernation Bay C-7
properties.description: A small chamber with four hibernation pods arranged in a square. Two are sealed and frosted over, their occupants dimly visible through the glass. One stands empty, its lid raised, interior dry. The fourth — yours — is open and still trailing cold vapor. Emergency strip lighting casts everything in pale amber. A status panel on the wall blinks. The air smells faintly of antiseptic.
properties.aiPrompt: This is the starting room. The goal is to introduce the player gently — a small, contained space that orients them before they step into the larger ship. Keep it simple: the pod they woke from, a couple of sealed pods with sleeping people (context, not interactive), and the status panel. Don't add too much here — the interesting stuff is out in the ship.

scenery.description: Your hibernation pod is a sleek, form-fitting shell of composite polymer, still radiating a deep, lung-chilling frost. The interior padding bears the lingering impression of your body, surrounded by a tangle of disconnected bio-monitoring leads that look like severed white vines.
scenery.rejection: The pod has fulfilled its purpose; there is no reason to climb back into that freezing coffin.

properties.name: Hibernation Bay C-7
properties.description: A small chamber with four hibernation pods arranged in a square. Two are sealed and frosted over, their occupants dimly visible through the glass. One stands empty, its lid raised, interior dry. The fourth — yours — is open and still trailing cold vapor. Emergency strip lighting casts everything in pale amber. A status panel on the wall blinks. The air smells faintly of antiseptic.

Don't add too much here — the interesting stuff is out in the ship.

scenery.description: Your hibernation pod is a sleek, form-fitting shell of composite polymer, still radiating a deep, lung-chilling frost. The interior padding bears the lingering impression of your body, surrounded by a tangle of disconnected bio-monitoring leads that look like severed white vines.
scenery.rejection: The pod has fulfilled its purpose; there is no reason to climb back into that freezing coffin.

scenery.description: Four sleek, casket-like units occupy the center of the bay, their white composite shells showing faint discolorations where hands have brushed against them for centuries. The two sealed units hum with a low, rhythmic vibration, while your own smells of ozone and the lingering chill of liquid nitrogen.
scenery.rejection: The magnetic seals on the occupied units are locked tight by a ship-wide protocol you don't yet understand.

properties.name: hibernation pods
properties.description: Four pods are arranged here. Two remain sealed with a thick layer of frost obscuring the occupants, while the other two stand open—one being the unit you just vacated, still smelling of medicinal preservatives.
properties.takeRefusal: The pods are heavy-duty medical installations, bolted deep into the deck plating and connected to the ship's life support veins.

properties.name: Corridor C
properties.description: A broad corridor with gently curved walls of brushed metal. Colored guide strips run along the floor — green toward the bow, red toward the stern. Recessed lighting panels overhead flicker intermittently, some dead entirely. A faded mural on one wall depicts people planting trees under a stylized sun. Below it, someone has scratched smaller figures in a different style.
properties.aiPrompt: The first step into the wider ship. The player should start noticing that this place has layers — the mural and the scratched figures hint that different people lived here at different times. This corridor connects the safe starting area to the rest of the deck. Objects here should be transitional: signage, wall markings, small hints of the ship's history.

scenery.description: The mural's original layer is a masterpiece of geometric precision, showing people in matching jumpsuits tending to a lush, green world. Overlapping the bottom edge, a series of frantic, charcoal-like scratches depict stick figures dancing around a fire that looks suspiciously like a malfunctioning engine core.
scenery.rejection: The pigment is baked directly into the bulkhead's polymer coating; you couldn't scrub it off even if you had the heart to try.

properties.name: Pulsing Pith-Fruit
properties.description: A translucent, pear-shaped fruit that seems to beat with a slow, rhythmic internal light. Its skin is patterned with bioluminescent veins that suggest a nervous system more than a nutrient network.

properties.name: Synthetic Apple
properties.description: A perfectly spherical fruit with a skin that shimmers like brushed copper. It smells faintly of ozone and cinnamon, a product of the ship's ancient nutrient synthesizers.
